text:
There once was a boy named Jack who lived beside an enchanted forest. Every morning, birds sang and dewdrops glistened on the leaves. Jack loved nature more than anything and often dared his best friend, Oliver, to trek through the forest.

One sunlit morning, Oliver insisted they discover the deepest part of the woodland. They pushed through brambles and climbed vines, feeling proud as they reached a clearing bathed in golden sunlight.

Suddenly, a deep voice boomed from the heart of the forest.

"Sabber?"

Jack knew it was his dear owl friend. Owlish owl drooped lazily from a branch.

"Hey, Sabber," Oliver said. "You OK?"

"A bit lost," Owlish replied. "Where do birds go?"

"Oh, we go to perch on lofty things!" insisted Jack.

They saw a flock scattering upwards. Up climbed they after them! Birds flashed vibrant plumages against azure skies. They soared high through leaves and clouds.

Above them,
